behaviors.description: An image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is model judgment quality: whether explicit imagery is actually detected. A mocked classifier would hide misclassification failures and pass trivially. Rejected bogus shortcut: choosing Integration just because the hold/publish wiring is testable with stubs. Rejected higher level Workflow because behavior text covers a single model decision path ("An image ... is flagged"), not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run repeated submissions of a labeled explicit-image set through the real vision moderation model and assert each item is held (not published), including the downstream hold-state write.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires real model outputs; lower-level mocked tests cannot catch true miss-classification.

behaviors.description: A text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (e.g., letter substitution, unicode lookalikes).
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on robustness of real text-model semantics under adversarial obfuscation, not just deterministic parsing logic. A stubbed model would always pass and miss the actual failure. Rejected bogus shortcut: choosing lowest level because obfuscation normalization can be unit-tested; the behavior explicitly targets model detection quality. Rejected Workflow because only one agentic classification decision is required per post.
behaviors.test_description: Evaluate an adversarial corpus of obfuscated hate-speech samples against the real text moderation model and assert they are flagged/held at required recall threshold.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The assertion is about model output quality; deterministic doubles are insufficient.

behaviors.description: A post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is local orchestration/state handoff: combining two check results and executing publish path correctly. The behavior text ("passes both content checks") gives model outcomes as preconditions, so correctness does not require evaluating real AI output quality. Rejected bogus shortcut: Integration by default for multi-module features; kept Integration only after brake checks: no required OS/network/subprocess behavior, no dependence on model-content correctness, no required multi-agentic decision chain for this assertion. Rejected higher Agentic/Workflow because the behavior does not ask whether models judged correctly, only whether publish routing follows pass results.
behaviors.test_description: Wire moderation orchestrator + repository/queue/publisher with deterministic pass results from both check modules and assert post state transitions to published (not held, not dropped).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: A unit test of one function is too narrow; integration wiring is needed to catch silent-drop handoff bugs.

behaviors.description: The full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision — completes within 5 seconds of submission.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires end-to-end timing across multiple agentic steps in sequence (vision call, text call, decision). Mocking either model hides real latency/interactions; this is not just module wiring. Rejected bogus shortcut: System-only because there is real infra; Workflow is required since timing depends on chained real AI calls. Rejected lower Agentic because a single model call does not represent the full sequential pipeline budget.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end submissions through real image and text moderation APIs with production-like concurrency and measure p95/p99 completion from submit to final publish/hold decision, asserting <=5s SLO.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Lower-level tests cannot expose cumulative latency and sequencing effects of multiple real model calls.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved.
behaviors.justification: This behavior targets fallback orchestration (error -> queue retry) rather than model-output quality. Deterministic failure injection from the vision client is sufficient to catch the core bug (accidental auto-approve). Rejected bogus shortcut: choosing Unit because retry logic exists in one method; real failure appears at module boundary between model client, decision service, and queue. Rejected higher System/Agentic: behavior text is about unavailability handling, not real OS/network semantics or AI judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire moderation service with queue/repository and a failing vision-client stub (timeout/503/error), then assert image posts transition to retry queue and never to approved/published state.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration is the minimum that exercises cross-module error propagation into queueing.

behaviors.description: The moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window.
behaviors.justification: Behavior requires repeated agentic decisions over time and consistency across submissions, i.e., multiple real model-involved steps plus state/caching handoff. A mock returning fixed labels would always pass and hide nondeterminism-related failures. Rejected bogus shortcut: choosing Agentic for a single-call quality check; text explicitly requires sequence ("submitted multiple times within a short window"). Rejected Integration because model decision variability is central to the failure mode.
behaviors.test_description: Submit the same borderline image repeatedly within the target window through the real moderation pipeline, assert decision consistency policy is met across attempts, and verify any cache/sticky-decision mechanism applies correctly.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test inherently depends on repeated real model behavior and cross-attempt orchestration.
